其實高安基究竟想點?

買隊波番黎,
唔注資，唔理球會成績，唔參與球會日常事務，淨係決定同雲加續約…
好啦，而家有人肯出多一倍市價買晒佢手上D股份，佢又唔制！

高安基舊年喺「球隊班主的進化」的討論度講，球隊如果想贏取錦標，班主最好完全不要插手其中，只要當成公司一樣好好經營便已是幫了最大的忙。

高安基和雲加是很好的拍檔，高安基也對雲加非常滿意。這也難怪，雲加以最少的支出，帶來球會在盈利和財政方面的強大表現，完全符合高安基管理和經營一家公司的期望。

更甚的是，球迷對成績，甚至其他不滿的抗議，有雲加作為發洩目標和主要對象，極少露面的高安基樂得躲在幕後避開所有指責，讓雲加充當擋箭牌，那又何樂而不為？所以在酋長球場裡，請雲加退休的banner，曝光率遠比請高安基離開的banner高。高安基就只是每年有一天在會員大會面對球迷，其餘時間就靠雲加去承受球迷壓力。結果，雲加得到班主不會過問和無條件支持的優良待遇，而班主則樂得擁有一位明瞭經濟原理，能配合他以經營生意的手法去管理球隊的領隊。

Evans will only be allowed to leave The Hawthorns once the Baggies have a replacement.
Their preferred target is Manchester City's Eliaquim Mangala and with Manchester City the preferred destination for Evans, that deal would appear straightforward.
However, Mangala would prefer to join Inter Milan.
To further complicate matters, Inter's prime target is thought to be Arsenal's Shkodran Mustafi.
But the Gunners will only sanction the departure of Mustafi if they have a replacement, ideally Evans.
But, as mentioned, West Brom will not let Evans go unless they have a replacement - ideally Mangala - and Manchester City will not let Mangala join West Brom unless they get Evans.
Get all that?

左翼最有機會暫時係：

2.伊禾比 我知大家會好失望 唔想聽到佢個名/見到佢出現喺正選位置 但現實係宜家咁遲嚟買人 有一定困難 如果丁力冇涉及喺山狗單交易入面 好不幸地 佢就會係左翼第一人選

呢幾場踢到冇哂信心 應該有排都未打得翻起

兩個都有自己心儀球會 前者心繫利記 後者鍾情於鏝 諗唔到有咩理由嚟我哋垃圾阿

所以總括我認為呢幾日要入中堅難上加難 要怪就怪老嘢點解要賣走G5啦

Nigel Winterburn has branded Arsenal's performance at Liverpool as “unacceptable.”

Winterburn, in an interview with talkSPORT, said: “Firstly, being associated with the club, I need to apologise to the supporters.

“I was there and I really did feel for them. People know I work for the club but this morning I am a little bit lost for words.

“I cannot believe what I saw. It was totally unacceptable for an Arsenal team.

“Usually I look and try and be constructive and you try and relate back to situations when you have been put in that position yourself, but every component of the team broke down very, very quickly.

“Once Liverpool created that first chance after about 10, maybe 15 minutes, we just seemed to panic and we were just waiting for a goal to come.

“The pressure was building, the midfield was so open. We just couldn’t cope with any aspect of Liverpool’s game and that was very, very disappointing.

“I’m sure Arsene Wenger and his backroom staff would have worked with the team throughout the week about how they wanted to play but it just seemed to collapse very, very quickly.”
